hook: it looked like someone had hidden a giant pearl inside the abdomen
whatYouSee: A large, well-defined rounded calcified mass in the right upper quadrant, sitting in the region of the gallbladder fossa.
whyItMatters: Stones this size can erode into the bowel and cause a gallstone ileus, a mechanical obstruction that is easy to miss and dangerous if delayed.
treatment: Cholecystectomy, usually laparoscopic; if the stone has already obstructed the bowel it is surgically removed (enterolithotomy).
takeaway: Most gallstones are radiolucent, so a stone big and dense enough to show plainly on plain film deserves a second look for complications.
igOptions: A. Foreign body the patient swallowed

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with abdominal discomfort.
Then the X-ray loaded 😭
And it looked like someone had hidden a giant pearl inside the abdomen.
Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻
What's the most likely diagnosis?
Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Giant Gallstone
👀 What you see:
A large, well-defined rounded calcified mass in the right upper quadrant, sitting in the region of the gallbladder fossa.
🦴 Why it matters:
Stones this size can erode into the bowel and cause a gallstone ileus, a mechanical obstruction that is easy to miss and dangerous if delayed.
💊 Treatment:
Cholecystectomy, usually laparoscopic; if the stone has already obstructed the bowel it is surgically removed (enterolithotomy).
📝 Takeaway:
Most gallstones are radiolucent, so a stone big and dense enough to show plainly on plain film deserves a second look for complications.
